title: Malaria Early Warning Signals In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where rainfall, temperature, and vegetation signals may provide usable lead time for sub-national malaria early warning in Sub-Saharan Africa, without pretending that a model output replaces local operational judgment.
why_it_matters: Malaria kills over 600,000 people annually, mostly children under five in Sub-Saharan Africa. Climate-informed early warning could save lives if signals are verified and uncertainty is communicated honestly. False certainty or unvalidated forecasts risk diverting resources and eroding trust.

success_metrics.target: Every candidate climate signal links to a dated evidence record with method and limitations.
success_metrics.validation_method: Run schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

success_metrics.target: No alert threshold or intervention recommendation is merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status, review records, and safety flags before accepting outputs.md updates.

success_metrics.target: Candidate analyses preserve the finest geographic grain that source data permits.
success_metrics.validation_method: Review reproducibility steps and confirm aggregation choices in pull requests.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review for operational claims, and replication for quantitative outputs.
